reviews.review_title: YO HO HO,  A PIRATES  LIFE FOR ME
reviews.review_description:
Waited patiently for this to become available, very excited for this game, I love a pirate themed game, so far I think it’s just excellent, climbing is on point and the graphics look so good on the Quest 3.
PSVR players moaned about Rango, leave the parrot alone, he is my companion and not annoying…yet anyway. This is going to be a great adventure, thanks to the devs for porting this over to Quest.

Update. I have now completed the game
5 chapters, some really fun missions and slightly easy puzzles, my only criticism is, I wish it was 10 chapters, there was so much more that could have been done, explore a ship, buy a sword from a dodgy pirate, overall I enjoyed my time, thanks to the dev for the key, just wanted more.

developer_response.body:
Arrr, this warms our pirate hearts!
We’re mighty glad ye be enjoyin’ the adventure, and we’ll be sure to tell the parrot he’s earned a loyal crewmate. 🦜

reviews.review_title: Pls don't listen to other bad reviews
reviews.review_description:
If i have to be totally honest im probably not the only one who thinks there is not enough pirate games that are actually good. But this game is the solution. The good things and bad things will be listed below 

good things: beautiful green areas, funny, cool, and good mechanics and story.

Bad things: pretty bad voice acting, just chapter three in general, enemy health, and main character exaggerates to much.

but other than that it is the best pirate vr game ever made.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind words, it means the world to us!

reviews.review_description: The visuals of this game are amazing, and it's a fun, immersive journey with great pacing and some challenging puzzles. But very doable, albeit frustrating at some parts until you have a much needed ah-ha moment that makes you feel silly for not figuring it out sooner. I didn't know what to expect with a "pirate game", and agree that it would be fun to explore other areas of that world like the pirate ship, but that does not take away from this game and would recommend it to anyone wanting a low stress journey to a different world.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind words!

reviews.review_description: I guess my expectations were too high as I thought as a pirates game, there would at least be some ship or boat interaction but this is purely a survival game and the entire map is on an island where you’re looking for treasure. You’re accompanied by a humoursly rude parrot that guides you to the next point. However, be sure to keep it constantly in your peripjeral because once you lose it, it’s easy to get lost in the map. When it does, you can still hear it but sounds very close when actually is far away at the next point. Visually, the landscape setting is absolutely mesmerizing. Lush trees and clear blue sea. Up close, green hell quality but the general landscape is sensational. Mechanic and immersion wise, much is to be desired. When grabbing treasure, you have to click the grip button twice for it to be converted to gold. I would naturally try to put items in my backpack but you can’t grab the backpack from your shoulder like most games. It just pops up. When swimming, you don’t feel or hear like you’re swimming. Feels like more you’re just gliding along. The standard up or down motion, or joystick doesn’t adjust your climb or drop. I’ve died a few times when gripping the green moss looking spots that you’re supposed to on rocks and trees, even though physically I didn’t let go. Besides these minor bugs, this game is a pretty cool adventure! Climb rock and trees, slide down vines, encounters with leopards, and finding treasure! Fair warning, don’t click the menu button on your controllers or you restart at the closest checkpoint..

developer_response.body:
Thank ye kindly for the detailed review! 🏴‍☠️
We’ve taken notes and will be keepin’ it in mind as we continue to improve the experience on these cursed shores.

May yer climbs be steady and yer pockets full o’ gold!

reviews.review_description: It’s a super mid game..  all these people saying the graphics are so good either have t played a VR game before or they are lying.  Saw some that said the climbing is fun.  It’s not.  It’s the most basic climbing mechanism for any Vr game.  Really just boring with blocky enviornments and basic controls

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sharrring your thoughts! Sorry the adventure didn’t quite hit the mark for ye. 🏴‍☠️
Feedback like this helps us keep improving the experience for all hands on deck.

reviews.review_description: Wanted to love this but theres still a lot of things to be fixed. I’m only at chapter 3 and can’t be bothered to try and finish it. Items are difficult to pick up, storing stuff is not intuitive. I’ve been stuck in the same place trying to kill unlimited skeletons with very limited resources (which is only a light that I have to charge? Why not a sword, Am I not a pirate?) swimming is awkward. It’s got a lot of potential, hopefully the developers will keep updating the game. I couldn’t even tell if I’m in a tutorial still or the whole game is me following the parrot checkpoint to checkpoint and I’m too fed up and motion sick to continue. Devs please consider adjusting for motion sickness.

developer_response.body:
Hello, thanks for leaving us your thoughts! That’s absolutely not the experience we want players to have. Your points are all noted and passed to the devs.

We’re continuing to update and improve the game, and feedback like this truly helps.

reviews.review_description:
Graphics were excellent, length of game good for price, physics were good, e.g. shooting gun, aiming lantern fire. I did get to kill an enemy with a sword once.  Save points were good.  Oil and ammo were plentiful but still had to manage use.   The character voices were good, except of course for the annoying parrot.  I forgot to try and shoot him at the end. 

I lost my gun a few times and had to quit the game and restart for it to reappear.   The end was a little anti-climactic.  Would have loved to have held some of the found treasure.  It would have been great if game ended standing on the ship in sight, ready to set sail.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! Appreciate it!

reviews.review_description:
I give this ZERO stars, if I could ....

A unceasing, tedious and needlessly difficut tutorial.

I actually had to pause it, use the Quest browser and look at a YouTube walkthrough to find out wtf was going on ....

Not good. 

Then after the neverending rockclimbing, there was less in "The Climb", the Collect-A-Thon begins.

Wow, I feel like a pirate already ....

I had to restart EIGHT TIMES because I lost signt of the parrot you hafta follow.

When the tutorial that's supposed to TELL YOU HOW TO PLAY starts out almost unplayably difficult, welp, then it's time to  hang it up.

Thing is, since I wasted so much time on the tutorial, I went over the two-hour window to secure a refund ....  

The good reviews here?  In all likelihood bought & paid-for ....

Avoid like the Plague it is and get "Sail!" instead.

🤮🤮🤮

developer_response.body:
Shiver me timbers! We're sorry the game didn't quite click for ye, andwe truly appreciate you sharing such detailed feedback.

We know the opening and following the parrot can feel tricky at first — birds do be like that, flying off faster than expected, so it’s always best to keep an eye on where it’s heading. That said, we understand how this can impact the early experience, and we’ll definitely keep your feedback in mind as we continue working on improvements and updates.

We hope you might consider giving the adventure another go after future patches, and they will change your mind about the Pirate adventures!

reviews.review_title: A great idea for Risen fans!
reviews.review_description: A few drawbacks for me is there is still lagging in the frame rate. Also when pulling objects up into your hands is still buggy. Also the textures isn’t up to quest3 and 3s standards. This easily could be put on the quest 2 as well, meaning it’s not that advanced for it to be a quest3 and 3s title only. If they fix these things then I’ll update with 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Thank ye for the honest feedback, matey! 🏴‍☠️
We’ve taken note of the performance and interaction issues ye mentioned and truly appreciate ye pointing them out. We’re already workin’ on making the adventure smoother, and an update should roll out soon!

reviews.review_description: Visually stunning and immersive! Love being in this pirate world in VR. Levels are varied and the checkpoint system is very nice! Finding treasure is a nice touch. Would love to see Quest Achievements added...🏆

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review!

reviews.review_title: Indiana Jones and the Talkative Parrot
reviews.review_description:
It feels like the developers of this game have nailed the ideal VR balance. It’s not very long, but it’s beautiful, polished, and packed with moments. It’s longer than the early, bare-bones VR titles, yet short enough to be genuinely enjoyable without wearing you out.

VR doesn’t need massive 20-hour games that cost $100. What works here are focused, well-paced adventures for $15. And this game earns every single one of those dollars.

developer_response.body: Arrr, this is music to our ears! Thank ye so much for the review!

reviews.review_description:
This game looks great and adventurous so far but this goes out to the developers

                                     ⬇️⬇️
  
" P.S. this goes up to the developers of this game. Would you please fix the problem where when I'm walking it seems like I'm walking too fast in which I can't enjoy the scenery throughout the game as well. Please fix the problem asap. When this problem is resolved I will delete all this out and give it a better review that it deserves. Thank you

" I hope there's a lot more combat, battle ships and a very long game with it as well. 
It still has little bugs in it and when I walk it goes too fast. 
It's almost as if you have to be  careful with your remote so you won't have to walk so fast.
 please fix that issue as well. 
 it is a Early Access so hopefully they do a lot of touch-ups and more add-ons with more combats in it. 
Thanks

developer_response.body:
Arrr, thank ye for the kind words and helpful feedback, matey! 🏴‍☠️
We’re glad ye be enjoyin’ the adventure so far, and we’ve taken note of yer thoughts for the future!

reviews.review_description: just what i wanted it to be. A really good pirates game  Looking for lost treasure, and on the meta quest it looks good , im super happy to have played this game. Its well worth your time and treasure 💲  i read some of the not so good reviews and almost didn't get this game, im glade i didn't listen to them.

developer_response.body: Glad ye trusted yer pirate instincts and gave the game a g! May yer treasure stay shiny forever! Arrr! 🏴‍☠️

reviews.review_title: Pieces of 8, a Swashbuckling VR Treasure
reviews.review_description:
Only played enough to do a review but so far so good.  The visuals are lovely, with lush islands, atmospheric caves, and that classic pirate vibe done just right. Exploration feels rewarding, hanging off vines, sliding down ropes and such like, and the gameplay keeps you immersed the whole time finding hidden treasures and chests with the help of a witty but rude parrot.

Combat and interactions feel solid so far, and the sense of presence—climbing, fighting, and uncovering secrets feels awesome.

If you love story-driven VR, pirates, or just want an adventure that feels polished and immersive, Jolly Roger is an easy recommendation. Hoist the sails—this one’s a gem!

developer_response.body: Arrr, many thanks for the kind words, matey! 🏴‍☠️ May yer pockets stay heavy with the POE!

reviews.review_description:
Been waiting a while for this to release, and it finally came out a few days ago.
I played this from beginning to end… enjoyed every moment of it… first level was a little frustrating as I’m no good at climbing… but once past that, my frustration eased.
The graphics are very good on this, nice lighting effects… love the skeletons! 
The sounds, speech and music are very nice too.

The game is a little short, but I couldn’t stop from beginning to end… was addictive and had that “just one more go” affect on me.

Some nice puzzles are in here, some surprises, along with some combat to go along with the story.
But I do wish it was a little longer. 
Gameplay is between 2-4 hours.

It is a nice little game, and gets you thinking at times with the puzzles… so I would recommend it, as it’s a great game with a lot going for it… and if you are like me, you prefer games that are shorter rather than drag on for too long

developer_response.body: We’re mighty glad ye enjoyed the adventure from start to finish! Thanks so much!

reviews.review_description:
I'm enjoying this game a lot but the pickaxe had no weight to it and just kinda clipped though the rocks as I hit them. Graphics are incredible though, the island the caves swimming underwater finding treasure the parrot all that is incredible and the price is really cheap for a game of this quality! It anyone has trouble finding the head of the pickaxe it's underwater btw. Now I gotta find the axe head. Its a proper adventure and it's pretty amazing you guys got this running on standalone hardware.

Edit: Finished the game. I really like how easily you can jump between the different parts of the game. I went straight back to the first part on the island and went around searching for all the treasure to unlock the climbing challenge and to just really take in the location. To me this IS the game.... this is what I bought...a lovely Caribbean location to walk, climb, swim, explore and find treasure. The whole adventure in the cave in an addon. I know some people say there's no point to finding the treasure but... what's the point in anything in a game? I wish i could get across to those little islands or swim out to the pirate ships a bit closer. Btw I found i could upscale the resolution 2x using QGO and it runs fine except when it's loading sections of the map. I noticed there's a cave I couldn't get into, makes me wonder if it was going to be an expansion. I'd love to buy some addon content... perhaps your ship floats away and you need to get to the other side of the island or something

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feefback, matey! We've noted everrrything down! Save and plentiful travels.

reviews.review_title: Broken boss battle at the end
reviews.review_description: Absolutely hate all these senseless boss battles of dumbness wish developers would get off these stupid fights my gun dissappear bs, I quit uninstall and will never play again was on the last chapter,  whatever on to better games .

developer_response.body: Hello, we've just released a patch that fixes the issue where the weapon disappears. Please update the game and let us know if it's all good now. Thanks so much for bringing this to our attention.

reviews.review_description: An amazing adventure! Really beautiful graphics, and not once did I encounter any insects. My only complaint is that it was far too short. The introduction suggests that you’re going to head out to one of the ships lying in the water. That’s why the disappointment was great when I suddenly found myself outside the cave, could see the ships again—but then the game was over. Apart from that, it was a good game.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks so much for the review! Our goal was to explore what a pirate’s life looks like on land, since most stories focus only on sea adventures. We wanted to offer a different perspective and try something a little unique. While the game may feel short, we’ve done our best to price it fairly to reflect the experience. :)))

reviews.review_title: So far It is very good
reviews.review_description: I am enjoying the experience. It is like a child fantasy come true. More games like this

developer_response.body: Thank you! Glad you like it!

reviews.review_description: Not that fun you ￼ would only like it if you’re desperate to find a pirate game ￼

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks for the feedback, sorry to hear that game doesn't meet your expectations

reviews.review_description:
It looks fantastic! I enjoyed the adventure. It was only a couple of hours, but I knew it wasn't going to be a long game with the low price. I would have paid more for it though. I did enjoy it very much. I didn't experience any bugs or glitches. It was smooth from start to finish. I would love to play a part 2!
I'm on the Quest 3s by the way.

developer_response.body: And you look fantastic too! Thanks for the review, much arrrprecieated! 🏴‍☠️

reviews.review_description: I only played an hour or so but the mechanics are good.  The graphics and sound are great.  I like what I see so far and will update when I complete it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! Glad you like it!

reviews.review_description: Whilst I enjoyed the opening, when I got control was when the game went downhill. It took a while to realise I could climb trees, the bird told me to follow him but I missed where he went. Then I got killed in a tree by a monkey I couldn't see. Just not fun. Refunded.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, sorry to hear that game doesn't meet your expectations

reviews.review_title: What a tease! Give us more!
reviews.review_description: Awesome game. Not long yes but, very fun and atmospheric. (No one is beating this in an hour so stop) Great job developers. Smooth, responsive. Only wish the grip/storage wasn’t allocated to a double trigger click. Otherwise, great adventure.

developer_response.body: Yarrr! Thanks so much for the kind words, matey! May yer pockets stay always full!

reviews.review_description:
Fun pirate game looks really good The controls are easy and the puzzles are enjoyable.
It’s not very long but it’s a good experience overall....If you like pirates and VR adventures you’ll enjoy it

developer_response.body: Arrr, glad ye enjoyed the adventure, matey! 🏴‍☠️

reviews.review_title: One hour and forty minutes game play
reviews.review_description: To short

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback!

reviews.review_description: Not without some niggles which I am sure will get fixed. Exploring is fun as is not knowing what comes next. Who doesn't like being a pirate?

developer_response.body: Arrr, glad ye enjoyed the adventure!🏴‍☠️

reviews.review_description: Garbage game

developer_response.body: Ahoy! Would ye mind telling us a bit more about your experience on the cursed shores? We love getting constructive feedback, it helps us set our route for updates and patches!

reviews.review_description: please get rid of the seaweed.

developer_response.body: We're sorry the game didn't quite click for you
